393	\section{Skyscraper sheaves are flasque}
394	\label{sec:H1Vanishing_skyscraperFlasque}
395	
396	\begin{lemma}
397	
398	\leanok
399	[Skyscraper sheaf as pushforward from a closed point]
400	  \label{lem:skyscraperSheaf_eq_pushforward}
401	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.skyscraperSheaf_eq_pushforward_const}
402	  % SOURCE: [Hartshorne], II.1, Exercise~1.17 (read from
403	  % references/hartshorne-algebraic-geometry.pdf, PDF page 68).
404	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Skyscraper Sheaves. Let \(X\) be a topological space,
405	  % let \(P\) be a point, and let \(A\) be an abelian group. Define a
406	  % sheaf \(i_P(A)\) on \(X\) as follows: \(i_P(A)(U) = A\) if \(P \in
407	  % U\), \(0\) otherwise. \ldots Show that this sheaf could also be
408	  % described as \(i_*(A)\), where \(A\) denotes the constant sheaf
409	  % \(A\) on the closed subspace \(\{P\}^-\), and \(i\colon \{P\}^- \to
410	  % X\) is the inclusion."
411	  \textit{Source: Hartshorne, II.1, Exercise~1.17.}
412	  Let \(X\) be a topological space, let \(P \in X\) be a point with
413	  closure \(\overline{\{P\}}\), and let \(A\) be a \(\bar k\)-module.
414	  Write \(i \colon \overline{\{P\}} \hookrightarrow X\) for the closed
415	  embedding. Then the skyscraper sheaf
416	  \(\mathtt{skyscraperSheaf}\;P\;A\) on \(X\) is naturally isomorphic to
417	  the pushforward \(i_*\bigl((\mathrm{constantSheaf}\;{-}\;\_)\;A\bigr)\)
418	  of the constant sheaf with value \(A\) on \(\overline{\{P\}}\).
419	\end{lemma}
420	
421	\begin{proof}
422	  Both presheaves send an open \(U \subseteq X\) to \(A\) when
423	  \(P \in U\) (equivalently, when
424	  \(U \cap \overline{\{P\}}\) is non-empty, since the closed subset
425	  \(\overline{\{P\}}\) is irreducible and any open meeting it contains
426	  \(P\)), and to \(0\) when \(P \notin U\) (equivalently,
427	  \(U \cap \overline{\{P\}} = \varnothing\)). The restriction maps agree
428	  by inspection: in the \(P \in V \subseteq U\) case both are the
429	  identity on \(A\); in the \(P \notin V\) case both factor through
430	  \(0\). The natural identification on opens is a sheaf morphism, and
431	  on stalks it is the identity (the stalk at any \(Q \neq P\) outside
432	  \(\overline{\{P\}}\) is zero on both sides; the stalk at any point of
433	  \(\overline{\{P\}}\) is \(A\) on both sides). Hence the comparison is
434	  an isomorphism of sheaves.
435	\end{proof}
436	
437	\begin{lemma}
438	
439	\leanok
440	[Closure of a closed point is irreducible]
441	  \label{lem:closedPoint_closure_irreducible}
442	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.PrimeDivisor.closure_isIrreducible}
443	
444	  Let \(C\) be a Noetherian scheme (in particular the smooth proper
445	  curve \(C / \bar k\) of this chapter), and let \(P \in C\) be a closed
446	  point. Then the closure \(\overline{\{P\}} = \{P\}\) is irreducible
447	  (trivially: a singleton is irreducible as a topological space).
448	
449	  \textit{Project-bespoke ancillary} (immediate set-theoretic fact;
450	  no external source pinned).
451	\end{lemma}
452	
453	\begin{proof}
454	
455	  A singleton topological space is irreducible: its only non-empty open
456	  is the whole space, which trivially intersects every other non-empty
457	  open in a non-empty set (in fact in itself). For a closed point
458	  \(P \in C\) the closure \(\overline{\{P\}}\) is \(\{P\}\), which is
459	  homeomorphic to a singleton, hence irreducible.
460	\end{proof}
461	
462	\begin{lemma}
463	
464	\leanok
465	[Closed-point skyscraper sheaf is flasque]
466	  \label{lem:skyscraperSheaf_isFlasque}
467	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.skyscraperSheaf_isFlasque}
468	  \uses{def:isFlasque_sheaf, lem:skyscraperSheaf_eq_pushforward,
469	        lem:closedPoint_closure_irreducible,
470	        lem:isFlasque_pushforward, lem:isFlasque_constant_irreducible}
471	
472	  Let \(C\) be a smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve over
473	  \(\bar k\) and let \(P \in C\) be a closed point. Then the
474	  closed-point skyscraper sheaf
475	  \[
476	    k(P) \;:=\; \mathtt{skyscraperSheaf}\;P\;
477	      (\Module.\mathtt{of}\;\bar k\;\bar k)
478	  \]
479	  is flasque as a sheaf of \(\bar k\)-modules on \(C\).
480	
481	  \textit{Project-bespoke packaging} (no separately named external
482	  source; the constituent inputs are quoted in their own lemma blocks
483	  above).
484	\end{lemma}
485	
486	\begin{proof}
487	  \uses{def:isFlasque_sheaf, lem:skyscraperSheaf_eq_pushforward, lem:closedPoint_closure_irreducible, lem:isFlasque_pushforward, lem:isFlasque_constant_irreducible}
488	  By
489	  \cref{lem:skyscraperSheaf_eq_pushforward}, the skyscraper sheaf is
490	  isomorphic to the pushforward
491	  \(i_*\bigl((\mathrm{constantSheaf}\;{-}\;\_)\;
492	  (\Module.\mathtt{of}\;\bar k\;\bar k)\bigr)\) along the inclusion
493	  \(i \colon \overline{\{P\}} \hookrightarrow C\). The base
494	  \(\overline{\{P\}} = \{P\}\) is irreducible
495	  (\cref{lem:closedPoint_closure_irreducible}), so the constant sheaf on
496	  it is flasque (\cref{lem:isFlasque_constant_irreducible}). Pushforward
497	  of a flasque sheaf is flasque
498	  (\cref{lem:isFlasque_pushforward}). Flasqueness transports along the
499	  isomorphism of \cref{lem:skyscraperSheaf_eq_pushforward}, so
500	  \(k(P)\) is flasque.
501	\end{proof}
502	
503	\section{\(H^1\) of a closed-point skyscraper sheaf vanishes}
504	\label{sec:H1Vanishing_skyscraperH1}
505	
506	\begin{theorem}
507	
508	\leanok
509	[\(H^1\) of a closed-point skyscraper sheaf has dimension zero]
510	  \label{lem:H1_skyscraperSheaf_finrank_eq_zero_main}
511	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.H1_skyscraperSheaf_finrank_eq_zero}
512	  \uses{thm:H1_vanishing_flasque, lem:skyscraperSheaf_isFlasque}
513	
514	  Let \(C\) be a smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve over
515	  \(\bar k\) and let \(P \in C\) be a closed point. Then
516	  \[
517	    \dim_{\bar k}\, H^1\bigl(C,\, \mathtt{skyscraperSheaf}\;P\;
518	      (\Module.\mathtt{of}\;\bar k\;\bar k)\bigr) \;=\; 0,
519	  \]
520	  where the left-hand side is the \(\bar k\)-finrank of
521	  \(\HModule\;\bar k\;(\mathtt{skyscraperSheaf}\;P\;(\Module.\mathtt{of}\;
522	  \bar k\;\bar k))\;1\).
523	
524	  \textit{Project-bespoke assembly} of the two substrate inputs of this
525	  chapter (\cref{thm:H1_vanishing_flasque} and
526	  \cref{lem:skyscraperSheaf_isFlasque}). The chapter's external source
527	  is Hartshorne~III.2 Proposition~2.5 applied to the flasque skyscraper
528	  sheaf — both inputs are quoted verbatim in their own lemma blocks
529	  above.
530	\end{theorem}
531	
532	\begin{proof}
533	  \uses{thm:H1_vanishing_flasque, lem:skyscraperSheaf_isFlasque}
534	  The closed-point skyscraper sheaf
535	  \(k(P) = \mathtt{skyscraperSheaf}\;P\;(\Module.\mathtt{of}\;\bar k\;
536	  \bar k)\) is flasque (\cref{lem:skyscraperSheaf_isFlasque}). By the
537	  flasque-cohomology vanishing theorem
538	  (\cref{thm:H1_vanishing_flasque}) applied at \(i = 1\) on the
539	  underlying topological space \(C.\mathtt{left.toTopCat}\),
540	  \[
541	    \HModule\;\bar k\;(k(P))\;1 \;=\; 0.
542	  \]
543	  Taking \(\Module.\mathtt{finrank}\;\bar k\) of the zero module gives
544	  the claimed identity \(\dim_{\bar k} H^1(C, k(P)) = 0\).
545	\end{proof}
546

630	\section{Out of scope}
631	
632	The following items lie outside this chapter and are scheduled into
633	sibling sub-builds:
634	\begin{itemize}
635	  \item \emph{General coherent-sheaf cohomology vanishing.} Higher
636	    cohomology vanishing for general coherent sheaves on a curve
637	    requires Grothendieck vanishing on a one-dimensional scheme
638	    (Hartshorne~III.2.7), which the chapter does \emph{not} use; the
639	    chapter is narrowly tailored to the flasque/skyscraper case
640	    \(H^1(C, k(P)) = 0\) that the genus-\(0\) critical path needs.
641	  \item \emph{Serre duality for curves.} The general-\(g\) Riemann--Roch
642	    statement \(\ell(D) - \ell(K - D) = \deg(D) + 1 - g\) routes through
643	    Serre duality \(H^1(C, \mathcal L) \cong H^0(C, \omega_C \otimes
644	    \mathcal L^{-1})^*\); Mathlib has neither the dualizing sheaf
645	    \(\omega_C\) on a scheme nor the duality theorem. The chapter
646	    sidesteps duality entirely by using flasqueness directly (the
647	    Hartshorne~III~\S2 order of presentation, not the
648	    Hartshorne~III~\S7 one).
649	  \item \emph{The \(H^0\) half of \(\chi(k(P)) = 1\).} The
650	    one-dimensionality identification \(\dim H^0(C, k(P)) = 1\) is the
651	    sibling substrate lemma
652	    REF of
653	    \cref{chap:RiemannRoch_RRFormula}, closed axiom-clean iter-188
654	    against the project's four-step \(\bar k\)-linear-equivalence chain.
655	    The present chapter is the complementary H\(^1\) half.
656	\end{itemize}

155:instance hasSheafCompose_of_preservesLimitsOfSize [PreservesLimitsOfSize.{v₁, max u₁ v₁} F] :

1	/-
2	Copyright (c) 2021 Adam Topaz. All rights reserved.
3	Released under Apache 2.0 license as described in the file LICENSE.
4	Authors: Adam Topaz
5	-/
6	module
7	
8	public import Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Sites.Sheaf
9	public import Mathlib.CategoryTheory.ConcreteCategory.Forget
10	
11	/-!
12	
13	In this file we construct the functor `Sheaf J A ⥤ Sheaf J B` between sheaf categories
14	obtained by composition with a functor `F : A ⥤ B`.
15	
16	In order for the sheaf condition to be preserved, `F` must preserve the correct limits.
17	The lemma `Presheaf.IsSheaf.comp` says that composition with such an `F` indeed preserves the
18	sheaf condition.
19	
20	The functor between sheaf categories is called `sheafCompose J F`.
21	Given a natural transformation `η : F ⟶ G`, we obtain a natural transformation
22	`sheafCompose J F ⟶ sheafCompose J G`, which we call `sheafCompose_map J η`.
23	
24	-/
25	
26	@[expose] public section
27	
28	
29	namespace CategoryTheory
30	
31	open CategoryTheory.Limits Functor
32	
33	universe v₁ v₂ v₃ u₁ u₂ u₃
34	
35	variable {C : Type u₁} [Category.{v₁} C]
36	variable {A : Type u₂} [Category.{v₂} A]
37	variable {B : Type u₃} [Category.{v₃} B]
38	variable (J : GrothendieckTopology C)
39	variable {U : C} (R : Presieve U)
40	variable (F G H : A ⥤ B) (η : F ⟶ G) (γ : G ⟶ H)
41	
42	/-- Describes the property of a functor to "preserve sheaves". -/
43	class GrothendieckTopology.HasSheafCompose : Prop where
44	  /-- For every sheaf `P`, `P ⋙ F` is a sheaf. -/
45	  isSheaf (P : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ A) (hP : Presheaf.IsSheaf J P) : Presheaf.IsSheaf J (P ⋙ F)
46	
47	variable [J.HasSheafCompose F] [J.HasSheafCompose G] [J.HasSheafCompose H]
48	
49	/-- Composing a functor which `HasSheafCompose`, yields a functor between sheaf categories. -/
50	@[simps! obj_obj map_hom]
51	def sheafCompose : Sheaf J A ⥤ Sheaf J B :=
52	  ObjectProperty.lift _
53	    (sheafToPresheaf _ _ ⋙ (Functor.whiskeringRight _ _ _).obj F)
54	      (fun P ↦ GrothendieckTopology.HasSheafCompose.isSheaf _ P.property)
55	
56	instance [F.Faithful] : (sheafCompose J F ⋙ sheafToPresheaf _ _).Faithful :=
57	  show (sheafToPresheaf _ _ ⋙ (whiskeringRight Cᵒᵖ A B).obj F).Faithful from inferInstance
58	
59	instance [F.Faithful] [F.Full] : (sheafCompose J F ⋙ sheafToPresheaf _ _).Full :=
60	  show (sheafToPresheaf _ _ ⋙ (whiskeringRight Cᵒᵖ A B).obj F).Full from inferInstance
61	
62	variable {F} in
63	/-- If `F : A ⥤ B` is fully faithful, then `sheafCompose J F ⋙ sheafToPresheaf J B` is fully
64	faithful. -/
65	def fullyFaithfulSheafComposeCompSheafToPresheaf (hF : F.FullyFaithful) :
66	    (sheafCompose J F ⋙ sheafToPresheaf J B).FullyFaithful :=
67	  (fullyFaithfulSheafToPresheaf J A).comp (hF.whiskeringRight Cᵒᵖ)
68	
69	instance [F.Faithful] : (sheafCompose J F).Faithful :=
70	  Functor.Faithful.of_comp (sheafCompose J F) (sheafToPresheaf _ _)
71	
72	instance [F.Full] [F.Faithful] : (sheafCompose J F).Full :=
73	  Functor.Full.of_comp_faithful (sheafCompose J F) (sheafToPresheaf _ _)
74	
75	variable {F} in
76	/-- If `F : A ⥤ B` is fully faithful, then `sheafCompose J F` is fully faithful. -/
77	def fullyFaithfulSheafCompose (hF : F.FullyFaithful) :
78	    (sheafCompose J F).FullyFaithful :=
79	  (fullyFaithfulSheafComposeCompSheafToPresheaf J hF).ofCompFaithful
80	
81	instance [F.ReflectsIsomorphisms] : (sheafCompose J F).ReflectsIsomorphisms where
82	  reflects {G₁ G₂} f _ := by
83	    rw [← isIso_iff_of_reflects_iso _ (sheafToPresheaf _ _),
84	      ← isIso_iff_of_reflects_iso _ ((whiskeringRight Cᵒᵖ A B).obj F)]
85	    change IsIso ((sheafToPresheaf _ _).map ((sheafCompose J F).map f))
86	    infer_instance
87	
88	variable {F G}
89	
90	/--
91	If `η : F ⟶ G` is a natural transformation then we obtain a morphism of functors
92	`sheafCompose J F ⟶ sheafCompose J G` by whiskering with `η` on the level of presheaves.
93	-/
94	def sheafCompose_map : sheafCompose J F ⟶ sheafCompose J G where
95	  app := fun _ => .mk <| whiskerLeft _ η
96	
97	@[simp]
98	lemma sheafCompose_id : sheafCompose_map (F := F) J (𝟙 _) = 𝟙 _ := rfl
99	
100	@[simp]
101	lemma sheafCompose_comp :
102	    sheafCompose_map J (η ≫ γ) = sheafCompose_map J η ≫ sheafCompose_map J γ := rfl
103	
104	namespace GrothendieckTopology.Cover
105	
106	variable (F G) {J}
107	variable (P : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ A) {X : C} (S : J.Cover X)
108	
109	/-- The multicospan associated to a cover `S : J.Cover X` and a presheaf of the form `P ⋙ F`
110	is isomorphic to the composition of the multicospan associated to `S` and `P`,
111	composed with `F`. -/
112	@[simps!]
113	def multicospanComp : (S.index (P ⋙ F)).multicospan ≅ (S.index P).multicospan ⋙ F :=
114	  NatIso.ofComponents
115	    (fun t =>
116	      match t with
117	      | WalkingMulticospan.left _ => Iso.refl _
118	      | WalkingMulticospan.right _ => Iso.refl _)
119	    (by
120	      rintro (a | b) (a | b) (f | f | f)
121	      all_goals cat_disch)
122	
123	/-- Mapping the multifork associated to a cover `S : J.Cover X` and a presheaf `P` with
124	respect to a functor `F` is isomorphic (upto a natural isomorphism of the underlying functors)
125	to the multifork associated to `S` and `P ⋙ F`. -/
126	def mapMultifork :
127	    F.mapCone (S.multifork P) ≅
128	      (Limits.Cone.postcompose (S.multicospanComp F P).hom).obj (S.multifork (P ⋙ F)) :=
129	  Cone.ext (Iso.refl _)
130	
131	end GrothendieckTopology.Cover
132	
133	/--
134	Composing a sheaf with a functor preserving the limit of `(S.index P).multicospan` yields a functor
135	between sheaf categories.
136	-/
137	instance (priority := high) hasSheafCompose_of_preservesMulticospan (F : A ⥤ B)
138	    [∀ (X : C) (S : J.Cover X) (P : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ A), PreservesLimit (S.index P).multicospan F] :
139	    J.HasSheafCompose F where
140	  isSheaf P hP := by
141	    rw [Presheaf.isSheaf_iff_multifork] at hP ⊢
142	    intro X S
143	    obtain ⟨h⟩ := hP X S
144	    replace h := isLimitOfPreserves F h
145	    replace h := Limits.IsLimit.ofIsoLimit h (S.mapMultifork F P)
146	    exact ⟨Limits.IsLimit.postcomposeHomEquiv (S.multicospanComp F P) _ h⟩
147	
148	/--
149	Composing a sheaf with a functor preserving limits of the same size as the hom sets in `C` yields a
150	functor between sheaf categories.
151	
152	Note: the size of the limit that `F` is required to preserve in
153	`hasSheafCompose_of_preservesMulticospan` is in general larger than this.
154	-/
155	instance hasSheafCompose_of_preservesLimitsOfSize [PreservesLimitsOfSize.{v₁, max u₁ v₁} F] :
156	    J.HasSheafCompose F where
157	  isSheaf _ hP := Presheaf.isSheaf_comp_of_isSheaf J _ F hP
158	
159	variable {J}
160	
161	lemma Sheaf.isSeparated {FA : A → A → Type*} {CA : A → Type*}
162	    [∀ X Y, FunLike (FA X Y) (CA X) (CA Y)] [ConcreteCategory A FA] [J.HasSheafCompose (forget A)]
163	    (F : Sheaf J A) : Presheaf.IsSeparated J F.obj := by
164	  rintro X S hS x y h
165	  exact (((isSheaf_iff_isSheaf_of_type _ _).1
166	    ((sheafCompose J (forget A)).obj F).2).isSeparated S hS).ext (fun _ _ hf => h _ _ hf)
167	
168	lemma Presheaf.IsSheaf.isSeparated {F : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ A} {FA : A → A → Type*} {CA : A → Type*}
169	    [∀ X Y, FunLike (FA X Y) (CA X) (CA Y)] [ConcreteCategory A FA]
170	    [J.HasSheafCompose (forget A)] (hF : Presheaf.IsSheaf J F) :
171	    Presheaf.IsSeparated J F :=
172	  Sheaf.isSeparated ⟨F, hF⟩
173	
174	end CategoryTheory

6	module

13	/-!
14	
15	# Limits and colimits of sheaves
16	
17	## Limits
18	
19	We prove that the forgetful functor from `Sheaf J D` to presheaves creates limits.
20	If the target category `D` has limits (of a certain shape),
21	this then implies that `Sheaf J D` has limits of the same shape and that the forgetful
22	functor preserves these limits.
23	
24	## Colimits
25	
26	Given a diagram `F : K ⥤ Sheaf J D` of sheaves, and a colimit cocone on the level of presheaves,
27	we show that the cocone obtained by sheafifying the cocone point is a colimit cocone of sheaves.
28	
29	This allows us to show that `Sheaf J D` has colimits (of a certain shape) as soon as `D` does.
30	
31	-/
32	
33	@[expose] public section
34	
35	
36	namespace CategoryTheory
37	
38	namespace Sheaf
39	
40	open CategoryTheory.Limits
41	
42	open Opposite
43	
44	universe w w' v u z z' u₁ u₂
45	
46	variable {C : Type u} [Category.{v} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
47	variable {D : Type w} [Category.{w'} D]
48	variable {K : Type z} [Category.{z'} K]
49	
50	section Limits

56	/-- An auxiliary definition to be used below.
57	
58	Whenever `E` is a cone of shape `K` of sheaves, and `S` is the multifork associated to a
59	covering `W` of an object `X`, with respect to the cone point `E.X`, this provides a cone of
60	shape `K` of objects in `D`, with cone point `S.X`.
61	
62	See `isLimitMultiforkOfIsLimit` for more on how this definition is used.
63	-/
64	def multiforkEvaluationCone (F : K ⥤ Sheaf J D) (E : Cone (F ⋙ sheafToPresheaf J D)) (X : C)
65	    (W : J.Cover X) (S : Multifork (W.index E.pt)) :
66	    Cone (F ⋙ sheafToPresheaf J D ⋙ (evaluation Cᵒᵖ D).obj (op X)) where
67	  pt := S.pt
68	  π :=
69	    { app := fun k => (Presheaf.isLimitOfIsSheaf J (F.obj k).1 W (F.obj k).2).lift <|
70	        Multifork.ofι _ S.pt (fun i => S.ι i ≫ (E.π.app k).app (op i.Y))
71	          (by
72	            intro i
73	            simp only [Category.assoc]
74	            erw [← (E.π.app k).naturality, ← (E.π.app k).naturality]
75	            dsimp
76	            simp only [← Category.assoc]
77	            congr 1
78	            apply S.condition)
79	      naturality := by
80	        intro i j f
81	        dsimp [Presheaf.isLimitOfIsSheaf]
82	        rw [Category.id_comp]
83	        apply Presheaf.IsSheaf.hom_ext (F.obj j).2 W
84	        intro ii
85	        rw [Presheaf.IsSheaf.amalgamate_map, Category.assoc, ← (F.map f).hom.naturality, ←
86	          Category.assoc, Presheaf.IsSheaf.amalgamate_map]
87	        erw [Category.assoc, ← E.w f]
88	        cat_disch }

99	def isLimitMultiforkOfIsLimit (F : K ⥤ Sheaf J D) (E : Cone (F ⋙ sheafToPresheaf J D))

132	then the limit presheaf is again a sheaf.
133	
134	This is used to show that the forgetful functor from sheaves to presheaves creates limits.
135	-/
136	theorem isSheaf_of_isLimit (F : K ⥤ Sheaf J D) (E : Cone (F ⋙ sheafToPresheaf J D))
137	    (hE : IsLimit E) : Presheaf.IsSheaf J E.pt := by
138	  rw [Presheaf.isSheaf_iff_multifork]
139	  intro X S
140	  exact ⟨isLimitMultiforkOfIsLimit _ _ hE _ _⟩

143	  limitsOfShape_le := by

145	    let F : K ⥤ Sheaf J D := ObjectProperty.lift _ h.diag h.prop_diag_obj
146	    exact isSheaf_of_isLimit F _ h.isLimit

159	end

173	    HasLimits (Sheaf J D) := inferInstance

175	end

182	
183	/-- Construct a cocone by sheafifying a cocone point of a cocone `E` of presheaves
184	over a functor which factors through sheaves.
185	In `isColimitSheafifyCocone`, we show that this is a colimit cocone when `E` is a colimit. -/
186	noncomputable def sheafifyCocone {F : K ⥤ Sheaf J D}
187	    (E : Cocone (F ⋙ sheafToPresheaf J D)) : Cocone F :=
188	  (Cocone.precompose
189	    (Functor.isoWhiskerLeft F (asIso (sheafificationAdjunction J D).counit).symm).hom).obj
190	    ((presheafToSheaf J D).mapCocone E)
191	
192	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
193	@[reassoc]
194	lemma sheafifyCocone_ι_app_val
195	    {F : K ⥤ Sheaf J D} (E : Cocone (F ⋙ sheafToPresheaf J D)) (k : K) :
196	    ((Sheaf.sheafifyCocone E).ι.app k).hom =
197	      E.ι.app k ≫ CategoryTheory.toSheafify J E.pt := by
198	  rw [← cancel_epi ((sheafToPresheaf _ _).map
199	    ((sheafificationAdjunction J D).counit.app (F.obj k)))]
200	  dsimp [sheafifyCocone]
201	  rw [← ObjectProperty.FullSubcategory.comp_hom_assoc,
202	    ← NatTrans.comp_app, IsIso.hom_inv_id, NatTrans.id_app]
203	  dsimp
204	  rw [Category.id_comp, toSheafify_naturality, sheafificationAdjunction_counit_app_val,
205	    sheafifyLift_id_toSheafify_assoc]
206	
207	/-- If `E` is a colimit cocone of presheaves, over a diagram factoring through sheaves,
208	then `sheafifyCocone E` is a colimit cocone. -/
209	noncomputable def isColimitSheafifyCocone {F : K ⥤ Sheaf J D}
210	    (E : Cocone (F ⋙ sheafToPresheaf J D)) (hE : IsColimit E) : IsColimit (sheafifyCocone E) :=
211	  (IsColimit.precomposeHomEquiv _ ((presheafToSheaf J D).mapCocone E)).symm
212	    (isColimitOfPreserves _ hE)
213	
214	instance [HasColimitsOfShape K D] : HasColimitsOfShape K (Sheaf J D) :=
215	  ⟨fun _ => HasColimit.mk
216	    ⟨sheafifyCocone (colimit.cocone _), isColimitSheafifyCocone _ (colimit.isColimit _)⟩⟩
217	
218	instance [HasFiniteCoproducts D] : HasFiniteCoproducts (Sheaf J D) :=
219	  ⟨inferInstance⟩
220

222	  ⟨fun _ ↦ inferInstance⟩
223	
224	instance [HasColimitsOfSize.{u₁, u₂} D] : HasColimitsOfSize.{u₁, u₂} (Sheaf J D) :=
225	  ⟨inferInstance⟩

228	/--
229	If every cocone on a diagram of sheaves which is a colimit on the level of presheaves satisfies
230	the condition that the cocone point is a sheaf, then the functor from sheaves to presheaves
231	creates colimits of the diagram.
232	Note: this almost never holds in sheaf categories in general, but it does for the extensive
233	topology (see `Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Sites/Coherent/ExtensiveColimits.lean`).
234	-/
235	@[implicit_reducible]
236	def createsColimitOfIsSheaf (F : K ⥤ Sheaf J D)
237	    (h : ∀ (c : Cocone (F ⋙ sheafToPresheaf J D)) (_ : IsColimit c), Presheaf.IsSheaf J c.pt) :
238	    CreatesColimit F (sheafToPresheaf J D) :=
239	  createsColimitOfReflectsIso fun E hE =>
240	    { liftedCocone := ⟨⟨E.pt, h _ hE⟩,
241	        ⟨fun _ => ⟨E.ι.app _⟩, fun _ _ _ => Sheaf.hom_ext <| E.ι.naturality _⟩⟩
242	      validLift := Cocone.ext (eqToIso rfl) fun j => by simp
243	      makesColimit :=
244	        { desc := fun S => ⟨hE.desc ((sheafToPresheaf J D).mapCocone S)⟩
245	          fac := fun S j => by ext1; dsimp; rw [hE.fac]; rfl
246	          uniq := fun S m hm => by
247	            ext1
248	            exact hE.uniq ((sheafToPresheaf J D).mapCocone S) m.hom fun j =>
249	              (ObjectProperty.ι _).congr_map (hm j) } }
250	
251	variable {D : Type w} [Category.{max v u} D]
252	
253	example [HasLimits D] : HasLimits (Sheaf J D) := inferInstance
254	
255	end Colimits
256	
257	end Sheaf

import Mathlib

open CategoryTheory CategoryTheory.Limits Functor

universe u v
variable {C : Type u} [Category.{v} C] {A : Type u} [Category.{v} A] {B : Type u} [Category.{v} B]

instance my_sheafCompose_preservesFiniteLimits (J : GrothendieckTopology C) 
    (F : A ⥤ B) [J.HasSheafCompose F] [HasFiniteLimits A] [HasFiniteLimits B]
    [PreservesFiniteLimits F] :
    PreservesFiniteLimits (sheafCompose J F) := by
  have h1 : (sheafCompose J F ⋙ sheafToPresheaf J B) =
      (sheafToPresheaf J A ⋙ (Functor.whiskeringRight _ _ _).obj F) := rfl
  -- the right side preserves finite limits
  have h2 : PreservesFiniteLimits (sheafToPresheaf J A ⋙ (Functor.whiskeringRight _ _ _).obj F) := by
    infer_instance
  rw [← h1] at h2
  -- sheafToPresheaf reflects isomorphisms, so it reflects limits
  apply preservesFiniteLimits_of_reflects_of_preserves _ (sheafToPresheaf J B)

import Mathlib

open CategoryTheory CategoryTheory.Limits Functor

universe u v
variable {C : Type u} [Category.{v} C] {A : Type u} [Category.{v} A] {B : Type u} [Category.{v} B]
  [HasFiniteLimits A] [HasFiniteLimits B] (J : GrothendieckTopology C)

-- the goal: build PreservesFiniteLimits on sheafCompose J F
example (F : A ⥤ B) [J.HasSheafCompose F] [PreservesFiniteLimits F] :
    PreservesFiniteLimits (sheafCompose J F) := by
  have heq : (sheafCompose J F ⋙ sheafToPresheaf J B) =
      (sheafToPresheaf J A ⋙ (Functor.whiskeringRight _ _ _).obj F) := rfl
  haveI hcomp : PreservesFiniteLimits (sheafCompose J F ⋙ sheafToPresheaf J B) := by
    rw [heq]
    exact ⟨fun K _ _ => inferInstance⟩
  exact preservesFiniteLimits_of_reflects_of_preserves _ (sheafToPresheaf J B)

105:/-- A `TopCat.Sheaf C X` is a presheaf of objects from `C` over a (bundled) topological space `X`,
128:def forget : TopCat.Sheaf C X ⥤ TopCat.Presheaf C X :=

100	
101	end Presheaf
102	
103	variable (C X)
104	
105	/-- A `TopCat.Sheaf C X` is a presheaf of objects from `C` over a (bundled) topological space `X`,
106	satisfying the sheaf condition.
107	-/
108	nonrec def Sheaf : Type max u v w :=
109	  Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology X) C
110	deriving Category
111	
112	variable {C X}
113	
114	/-- The underlying presheaf of a sheaf -/
115	abbrev Sheaf.presheaf (F : X.Sheaf C) : TopCat.Presheaf C X :=
116	  F.1
117	
118	variable (C X)
119	
120	-- Let's construct a trivial example, to keep the inhabited linter happy.
121	instance sheafInhabited : Inhabited (Sheaf (CategoryTheory.Discrete PUnit) X) :=
122	  ⟨⟨Functor.star _, Presheaf.isSheaf_unit _⟩⟩
123	
124	namespace Sheaf
125	
126	/-- The forgetful functor from sheaves to presheaves.
127	-/
128	def forget : TopCat.Sheaf C X ⥤ TopCat.Presheaf C X :=
129	  sheafToPresheaf _ _
130	
131	-- The following instances should be constructed by a deriving handler.
132	-- https://github.com/leanprover-community/mathlib4/issues/380
133	
134	instance forget_full : (forget C X).Full where
135	  map_surjective f := ⟨ObjectProperty.homMk f, rfl⟩
136	
137	instance forgetFaithful : (forget C X).Faithful where
138	  map_injective := Sheaf.hom_ext
139	
140	-- Note: These can be proved by simp.
141	theorem id_app (F : Sheaf C X) (t) : (𝟙 F : F ⟶ F).1.app t = 𝟙 _ :=
142	  rfl
143	
144	theorem comp_app {F G H : Sheaf C X} (f : F ⟶ G) (g : G ⟶ H) (t) :
145	    (f ≫ g).1.app t = f.1.app t ≫ g.1.app t :=
146	  rfl

import Mathlib

open CategoryTheory CategoryTheory.Limits Functor

universe u

-- Try writing the PreservesFiniteLimits instance via the factorization trick.
instance my_pf {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] {X : TopCat.{u}} :
    PreservesFiniteLimits (sheafCompose (Opens.grothendieckTopology X)
      (forget₂ (ModuleCat.{u} kbar) AddCommGrpCat.{u})) := by
  set Fforget := sheafCompose (Opens.grothendieckTopology X)
      (forget₂ (ModuleCat.{u} kbar) AddCommGrpCat.{u}) with hF
  set J := Opens.grothendieckTopology X with hJ
  -- factorization equality (already verified to hold via rfl)
  have heq : (Fforget ⋙ sheafToPresheaf J AddCommGrpCat.{u}) =
      (sheafToPresheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} kbar) ⋙
        (Functor.whiskeringRight _ _ _).obj (forget₂ (ModuleCat.{u} kbar) AddCommGrpCat.{u})) := rfl
  -- The right side preserves finite limits:
  haveI hcomp : PreservesFiniteLimits (Fforget ⋙ sheafToPresheaf J AddCommGrpCat.{u}) := by
    rw [heq]
    refine ⟨fun K _ _ => ?_⟩
    infer_instance
  -- sheafToPresheaf reflects all limits (fully faithful), so it reflects isomorphisms
  exact preservesFiniteLimits_of_reflects_of_preserves _ (sheafToPresheaf J AddCommGrpCat.{u})

content: Let me look at how I can manually construct `PreservesSheafification`.

1	/-
2	Copyright (c) 2024 Joël Riou. All rights reserved.
3	Released under Apache 2.0 license as described in the file LICENSE.
4	Authors: Joël Riou
5	-/
6	module
7	
8	public import Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Sites.Localization
9	public import Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Sites.CompatibleSheafification
10	public import Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Sites.Whiskering
11	public import Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Sites.Sheafification
12	
13	/-! # Functors which preserve sheafification
14	
15	In this file, given a Grothendieck topology `J` on `C` and `F : A ⥤ B`,
16	we define a type class `J.PreservesSheafification F`. We say that `F` preserves
17	the sheafification if whenever a morphism of presheaves `P₁ ⟶ P₂` induces
18	an isomorphism on the associated sheaves, then the induced map `P₁ ⋙ F ⟶ P₂ ⋙ F`
19	also induces an isomorphism on the associated sheaves. (Note: it suffices to check
20	this property for the map from any presheaf `P` to its associated sheaf, see
21	`GrothendieckTopology.preservesSheafification_iff_of_adjunctions`).
22	
23	In general, we define `Sheaf.composeAndSheafify J F : Sheaf J A ⥤ Sheaf J B` as the functor
24	which sends a sheaf `G` to the sheafification of the composition `G.val ⋙ F`.
25	If `J.PreservesSheafification F`, we show that this functor can also be thought of
26	as the localization of the functor `_ ⋙ F` on presheaves: we construct an isomorphism
27	`presheafToSheafCompComposeAndSheafifyIso` between
28	`presheafToSheaf J A ⋙ Sheaf.composeAndSheafify J F` and
29	`(whiskeringRight Cᵒᵖ A B).obj F ⋙ presheafToSheaf J B`.
30	
31	Moreover, if we assume `J.HasSheafCompose F`, we obtain an isomorphism
32	`sheafifyComposeIso J F P : sheafify J (P ⋙ F) ≅ sheafify J P ⋙ F`.
33	
34	We show that under suitable assumptions, the forgetful functor from a concrete
35	category preserves sheafification; this holds more generally for
36	functors between such concrete categories which commute both with
37	suitable limits and colimits.
38	
39	## TODO
40	* construct an isomorphism `Sheaf.composeAndSheafify J F ≅ sheafCompose J F`
41	
42	-/
43	
44	@[expose] public section
45	
46	universe v u
47	
48	namespace CategoryTheory
49	
50	open Category Limits Functor
51	
52	variable {C : Type u} [Category.{v} C] (J : GrothendieckTopology C)
53	  {A B : Type*} [Category* A] [Category* B] (F : A ⥤ B)
54	
55	namespace GrothendieckTopology
56	
57	/-- A functor `F : A ⥤ B` preserves the sheafification for the Grothendieck
58	topology `J` on a category `C` if whenever a morphism of presheaves `f : P₁ ⟶ P₂`
59	in `Cᵒᵖ ⥤ A` is such that becomes an iso after sheafification, then it is
60	also the case of `whiskerRight f F : P₁ ⋙ F ⟶ P₂ ⋙ F`. -/
61	class PreservesSheafification : Prop where
62	  le : J.W ≤ J.W.inverseImage ((whiskeringRight Cᵒᵖ A B).obj F)
63	
64	variable [PreservesSheafification J F]
65	
66	lemma W_of_preservesSheafification
67	    {P₁ P₂ : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ A} (f : P₁ ⟶ P₂) (hf : J.W f) :
68	    J.W (whiskerRight f F) :=
69	  PreservesSheafification.le _ hf
70	
71	variable [HasWeakSheafify J B]
72	
73	lemma W_isInvertedBy_whiskeringRight_presheafToSheaf :
74	    J.W.IsInvertedBy (((whiskeringRight Cᵒᵖ A B).obj F) ⋙ presheafToSheaf J B) := by
75	  intro P₁ P₂ f hf
76	  dsimp
77	  rw [← W_iff]
78	  exact J.W_of_preservesSheafification F _ hf
79	
80	end GrothendieckTopology
81	
82	section
83	
84	variable [HasWeakSheafify J B]
85	
86	/-- This is the functor sending a sheaf `X : Sheaf J A` to the sheafification
87	of `X.val ⋙ F`. -/
88	noncomputable abbrev Sheaf.composeAndSheafify : Sheaf J A ⥤ Sheaf J B :=
89	  sheafToPresheaf J A ⋙ (whiskeringRight _ _ _).obj F ⋙ presheafToSheaf J B
90	
91	variable [HasWeakSheafify J A]
92	
93	/-- The canonical natural transformation from
94	`(whiskeringRight Cᵒᵖ A B).obj F ⋙ presheafToSheaf J B` to
95	`presheafToSheaf J A ⋙ Sheaf.composeAndSheafify J F`. -/
96	@[simps!]
97	noncomputable def toPresheafToSheafCompComposeAndSheafify :
98	    (whiskeringRight Cᵒᵖ A B).obj F ⋙ presheafToSheaf J B ⟶
99	      presheafToSheaf J A ⋙ Sheaf.composeAndSheafify J F :=
100	  whiskerRight (sheafificationAdjunction J A).unit
101	    ((whiskeringRight _ _ _).obj F ⋙ presheafToSheaf J B)
102	
103	variable [J.PreservesSheafification F]
104	
105	instance : IsIso (toPresheafToSheafCompComposeAndSheafify J F) := by
106	  rw [NatTrans.isIso_iff_isIso_app]
107	  intro X
108	  dsimp
109	  simpa only [← J.W_iff] using J.W_of_preservesSheafification F _ (J.W_toSheafify X)

  --   * presheafToSheaf is left adjoint, preserves colimits

lemma isLocallyInjective_of_whisker (hH : CoverPreserving J K H)
    [H.IsCoverDense K] [IsLocallyInjective J (whiskerLeft H.op f)] : IsLocallyInjective K f where
  equalizerSieve_mem {X} a b h := by
    apply K.transitive (H.is_cover_of_isCoverDense K X.unop)
    intro Y g ⟨⟨Z, lift, m, fac⟩⟩
    rw [← fac, Sieve.pullback_comp]
    apply K.pullback_stable
    refine K.superset_covering (Sieve.functorPullback_pushforward_le H _) ?_
    refine K.superset_covering (Sieve.functorPushforward_monotone H _ ?_)
      (hH.cover_preserve <| equalizerSieve_mem J (whiskerLeft H.op f)
        (F.map m.op a) (F.map m.op b) ?_)
--
  ⟨fun _ ↦ isLocallyInjective_of_whisker J K H f hH,
    fun _ ↦ isLocallyInjective_whisker J K H f⟩

lemma isLocallySurjective_whisker [H.IsCocontinuous J K] [IsLocallySurjective K f] :
    IsLocallySurjective J (whiskerLeft H.op f) where
  imageSieve_mem a := H.cover_lift J K (imageSieve_mem K f a)

lemma isLocallySurjective_of_whisker (hH : CoverPreserving J K H)
    [H.IsCoverDense K] [IsLocallySurjective J (whiskerLeft H.op f)] : IsLocallySurjective K f where
  imageSieve_mem {X} a := by
    apply K.transitive (H.is_cover_of_isCoverDense K X)
    intro Y g ⟨⟨Z, lift, m, fac⟩⟩
    rw [← fac, Sieve.pullback_comp]
    apply K.pullback_stable
    have hh := hH.cover_preserve <| imageSieve_mem J (whiskerLeft H.op f) (G.map m.op a)
    refine K.superset_covering (Sieve.functorPullback_pushforward_le H _) ?_
    refine K.superset_covering (Sieve.functorPushforward_monotone H _ ?_) hh
    intro W q ⟨x, h⟩

import Mathlib

open CategoryTheory CategoryTheory.Limits Functor Presheaf

universe u

example {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] {X : TopCat.{u}}
    {P₁ P₂ : (TopologicalSpace.Opens X)ᵒᵖ ⥤ ModuleCat.{u} kbar} (f : P₁ ⟶ P₂)
    [IsLocallyInjective (Opens.grothendieckTopology X) f] :
    IsLocallyInjective (Opens.grothendieckTopology X)
      (whiskerRight f (forget₂ (ModuleCat.{u} kbar) AddCommGrpCat.{u})) := by
  -- Use forget_iff: locally injective iff after forget (to types)
  rw [← isLocallyInjective_forget_iff (D := AddCommGrpCat.{u})]
  -- (whiskerRight f forget₂) ⋙ forget = whiskerRight f (forget₂ ⋙ forget) = whiskerRight f (forget _)
  -- forget₂ ⋙ forget AddCommGrpCat = forget (ModuleCat) via HasForget₂
  have h := HasForget₂.forget_comp (C := ModuleCat.{u} kbar) (D := AddCommGrpCat.{u})
  -- h : forget₂ ⋙ forget = forget
  have heq : whiskerRight (whiskerRight f (forget₂ (ModuleCat.{u} kbar) AddCommGrpCat.{u}))
      (forget AddCommGrpCat.{u}) =
      whiskerRight f (forget (ModuleCat.{u} kbar)) := by
    rw [← whiskerRight_comp]
    congr 1
  rw [heq]
  -- locally injective after forget to types iff locally injective
  rw [isLocallyInjective_forget_iff (D := ModuleCat.{u} kbar)]
  infer_instance

    (Opens.grothendieckTopology X).PreservesSheafification
      (forget₂ (ModuleCat.{u} kbar) AddCommGrpCat.{u}) := by

  intro P₁ P₂ f hf

  obtain ⟨h_li, h_ls⟩ := hf
  show (Opens.grothendieckTopology X).W _

  refine ⟨?_, ?_⟩
  · -- IsLocallyInjective on whiskerRight f forget₂

        (whiskerRight (whiskerRight f (forget₂ (ModuleCat.{u} kbar) AddCommGrpCat.{u}))

  · -- IsLocallySurjective on whiskerRight f forget₂

import Mathlib

open CategoryTheory CategoryTheory.Limits Functor Presheaf

universe u

-- Provide a project-local instance for PreservesFiniteLimits (sheafCompose ... forget₂)
-- Then ask for PreservesHomology
instance my_sheafCompose_pf {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] {X : TopCat.{u}} :
    PreservesFiniteLimits (sheafCompose (Opens.grothendieckTopology X)
      (forget₂ (ModuleCat.{u} kbar) AddCommGrpCat.{u})) := by
  have heq :
    (sheafCompose (Opens.grothendieckTopology X)
        (forget₂ (ModuleCat.{u} kbar) AddCommGrpCat.{u}) ⋙
      sheafToPresheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology X) AddCommGrpCat.{u}) =
    (sheafToPresheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology X) (ModuleCat.{u} kbar) ⋙
      (Functor.whiskeringRight _ _ _).obj
        (forget₂ (ModuleCat.{u} kbar) AddCommGrpCat.{u})) := rfl
  haveI hcomp : PreservesFiniteLimits
      (sheafCompose (Opens.grothendieckTopology X)
        (forget₂ (ModuleCat.{u} kbar) AddCommGrpCat.{u}) ⋙
      sheafToPresheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology X) AddCommGrpCat.{u}) := by
    rw [heq]
    refine ⟨fun K _ _ => ?_⟩
    infer_instance
  exact preservesFiniteLimits_of_reflects_of_preserves _
    (sheafToPresheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology X) AddCommGrpCat.{u})

-- Try to see if there's some auto-instance now
example {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] {X : TopCat.{u}}
    {S : ShortComplex (Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology X) (ModuleCat.{u} kbar))}
    (hS : S.Exact) :
    (S.map (sheafCompose (Opens.grothendieckTopology X)
      (forget₂ (ModuleCat.{u} kbar) AddCommGrpCat.{u}))).Exact := by
  exact hS.map _

import Mathlib

open CategoryTheory CategoryTheory.Limits Functor Presheaf

universe u

-- I'll try a direct approach using `isLocallyInjective` via composing through `forget`.
-- Key fact: forget₂ ModuleCat AddCommGrpCat composed with forget AddCommGrpCat = forget ModuleCat.
example {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] {X : TopCat.{u}}
    {P₁ P₂ : (TopologicalSpace.Opens X)ᵒᵖ ⥤ ModuleCat.{u} kbar} (f : P₁ ⟶ P₂)
    (h_li : IsLocallyInjective (Opens.grothendieckTopology X) f) :
    IsLocallyInjective (Opens.grothendieckTopology X)
      (whiskerRight f (forget₂ (ModuleCat.{u} kbar) AddCommGrpCat.{u})) := by
  haveI := h_li
  -- locally injective iff locally injective after forget (to types)
  rw [← isLocallyInjective_forget_iff (D := AddCommGrpCat.{u})]
  -- whiskerRight (whiskerRight f forget₂) forget = whiskerRight f (forget₂ ⋙ forget)
  -- = whiskerRight f (forget ModuleCat)
  have heq : (whiskerRight (whiskerRight f (forget₂ (ModuleCat.{u} kbar) AddCommGrpCat.{u}))
      (forget AddCommGrpCat.{u})) =
      whiskerRight f (forget (ModuleCat.{u} kbar)) := by
    ext U y
    rfl
  rw [heq]
  infer_instance  -- IsLocallyInjective J (whiskerRight f (forget ModuleCat)) from h_li

import Mathlib

open CategoryTheory CategoryTheory.Limits Functor Presheaf

universe u

-- The full PreservesSheafification instance for our specific forget₂.
instance my_preservesSheafification {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] {X : TopCat.{u}} :
    (Opens.grothendieckTopology X).PreservesSheafification
      (forget₂ (ModuleCat.{u} kbar) AddCommGrpCat.{u}) := by
  refine ⟨?_⟩
  intro P₁ P₂ f hf
  rw [GrothendieckTopology.W_iff_isLocallyBijective] at hf
  obtain ⟨h_li, h_ls⟩ := hf
  show (Opens.grothendieckTopology X).W _
  rw [GrothendieckTopology.W_iff_isLocallyBijective]
  refine ⟨?_, ?_⟩
  · -- IsLocallyInjective
    haveI := h_li
    haveI : IsLocallyInjective (Opens.grothendieckTopology X)
        (whiskerRight f (forget (ModuleCat.{u} kbar))) := inferInstance
    have heq : (whiskerRight (whiskerRight f (forget₂ (ModuleCat.{u} kbar) AddCommGrpCat.{u}))
        (forget AddCommGrpCat.{u})) =
        whiskerRight f (forget (ModuleCat.{u} kbar)) := by
      ext U y
      rfl
    rw [← isLocallyInjective_forget_iff (D := AddCommGrpCat.{u}), heq]
    exact this
  · -- IsLocallySurjective
    haveI := h_ls
    haveI : IsLocallySurjective (Opens.grothendieckTopology X)
        (whiskerRight f (forget (ModuleCat.{u} kbar))) :=
      (isLocallySurjective_iff_whisker_forget _ _).mp h_ls
    have heq : (whiskerRight (whiskerRight f (forget₂ (ModuleCat.{u} kbar) AddCommGrpCat.{u}))
        (forget AddCommGrpCat.{u})) =
        whiskerRight f (forget (ModuleCat.{u} kbar)) := by
      ext U y
      rfl
    have := (isLocallySurjective_iff_whisker_forget _
      (whiskerRight f (forget₂ (ModuleCat.{u} kbar) AddCommGrpCat.{u})))
    rw [heq] at this
    exact this.mpr (by infer_instance)

import Mathlib

open CategoryTheory CategoryTheory.Limits Functor Presheaf

universe u

instance my_preservesSheafification {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] {X : TopCat.{u}} :
    (Opens.grothendieckTopology X).PreservesSheafification
      (forget₂ (ModuleCat.{u} kbar) AddCommGrpCat.{u}) := by
  refine ⟨?_⟩
  intro P₁ P₂ f hf
  rw [GrothendieckTopology.W_iff_isLocallyBijective] at hf
  obtain ⟨h_li, h_ls⟩ := hf
  show (Opens.grothendieckTopology X).W _
  rw [GrothendieckTopology.W_iff_isLocallyBijective]
  -- Goal: Locally inj + locally surj of `((whiskeringRight ...).obj forget₂).map f`
  -- Note `((whiskeringRight Cᵒᵖ A B).obj F).map f = whiskerRight f F` defeq
  change IsLocallyInjective _ (whiskerRight f _) ∧
    IsLocallySurjective _ (whiskerRight f _)
  refine ⟨?_, ?_⟩
  · haveI := h_li
    haveI : IsLocallyInjective (Opens.grothendieckTopology X)
        (whiskerRight f (forget (ModuleCat.{u} kbar))) := inferInstance
    have heq : (whiskerRight (whiskerRight f (forget₂ (ModuleCat.{u} kbar) AddCommGrpCat.{u}))
        (forget AddCommGrpCat.{u})) =
        whiskerRight f (forget (ModuleCat.{u} kbar)) := by
      ext U y
      rfl
    rw [← isLocallyInjective_forget_iff (D := AddCommGrpCat.{u}), heq]
    exact this
  · haveI := h_ls
    -- locally surjective whiskered with forget₂
    rw [isLocallySurjective_iff_whisker_forget (D := AddCommGrpCat.{u})]
    have heq : (whiskerRight (whiskerRight f (forget₂ (ModuleCat.{u} kbar) AddCommGrpCat.{u}))
        (forget AddCommGrpCat.{u})) =
        whiskerRight f (forget (ModuleCat.{u} kbar)) := by
      ext U y
      rfl
    rw [heq]
    exact (isLocallySurjective_iff_whisker_forget _ f).mp h_ls

290	Hartshorne II.1.16(b) bridge

295	
296	The bridge is **structural**: `sheafCompose J (forget₂ ModuleCat AddCommGrpCat)`
297	is additive (hence preserves zero morphisms) and is a functor between
298	the corresponding sheaf categories. Its action on a sheaf
299	`F : Sheaf J (ModuleCat kbar)` produces an `AddCommGrpCat`-valued
300	sheaf whose section-level data is the underlying additive group of
301	the original — so `Function.Surjective` of section-level maps transfers
302	trivially across the bridge.
303

305	`ModuleCat kbar` level) to a corresponding `S.ShortExact` after
306	`sheafCompose forget₂` — the structural skeleton is in place; what's

311	/-- Additive instance for `sheafCompose J F` when `F` is additive.
312	Lets us discharge `PreservesZeroMorphisms` for the

314	instance sheafCompose_additive {C : Type*} [CategoryTheory.Category C]
315	    (J : CategoryTheory.GrothendieckTopology C)
316	    {A B : Type*} [CategoryTheory.Category A] [CategoryTheory.Category B]
317	    [CategoryTheory.Preadditive A] [CategoryTheory.Preadditive B]
318	    (F : A ⥤ B) [F.Additive] [J.HasSheafCompose F] :
319	    (CategoryTheory.sheafCompose J F).Additive where
320	  map_add := by
321	    intro X Y f g
322	    apply (CategoryTheory.sheafToPresheaf J B).map_injective
323	    change CategoryTheory.Functor.whiskerRight (f + g).hom F =
324	      CategoryTheory.Functor.whiskerRight f.hom F +
325	      CategoryTheory.Functor.whiskerRight g.hom F
326	    ext c
327	    simp
328	    rfl

331	is additive (derived from the `Additive` instance above). -/
332	instance sheafCompose_preservesZero {C : Type*} [CategoryTheory.Category C]
333	    (J : CategoryTheory.GrothendieckTopology C)
334	    {A B : Type*} [CategoryTheory.Category A] [CategoryTheory.Category B]
335	    [CategoryTheory.Preadditive A] [CategoryTheory.Preadditive B]
336	    (F : A ⥤ B) [F.Additive] [J.HasSheafCompose F] :
337	    (CategoryTheory.sheafCompose J F).PreservesZeroMorphisms :=
338	  CategoryTheory.Functor.preservesZeroMorphisms_of_additive _
339	
340	/-- **`Scheme.IsFlasque` transfers to `TopCat.Sheaf.IsFlasque` under
341	`sheafCompose forget₂`** (axiom-clean structural helper).
342	
343	The forget₂ functor `ModuleCat kbar → AddCommGrpCat` is faithful and
344	preserves the underlying function of every morphism. So
345	`Function.Surjective` of the underlying section-level restriction map
346	transfers across to `Epi` in `AddCommGrpCat` (via
347	`AddCommGrpCat.epi_iff_surjective`), and `Scheme.IsFlasque F` (which
348	says all restrictions are sectionwise surjective) directly produces
349	the `TopCat.Sheaf.IsFlasque` (which says all restrictions are epi). -/
350	theorem Scheme.IsFlasque.toAddCommGrpCat
351	    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] {X : TopCat.{u}}
352	    {F : Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology X) (ModuleCat.{u} kbar)}
353	    (hF : Scheme.IsFlasque F) :
354	    TopCat.Sheaf.IsFlasque
355	      ((CategoryTheory.sheafCompose (Opens.grothendieckTopology X)
356	        (CategoryTheory.forget₂ (ModuleCat.{u} kbar) AddCommGrpCat.{u})).obj F) := by
357	  refine ⟨?_⟩
358	  intro U V i
359	  rw [AddCommGrpCat.epi_iff_surjective]
360	  intro y
361	  -- `i : U ⟶ V` in `(Opens X)ᵒᵖ` corresponds to `V.unop ≤ U.unop` in `Opens X`.
362	  have hle : V.unop ≤ U.unop := leOfHom i.unop
363	  obtain ⟨x, hx⟩ := hF hle y
364	  exact ⟨x, hx⟩
365	
366	/-- **Hartshorne II.1, Exercise 1.16(b)** (sections form): the
367	sheaf-morphism `S.g` is sectionwise-surjective on a short exact sequence
368	whose leftmost sheaf is flasque.
369	
370	For a sheaf-level short exact sequence
371	`0 → S.X₁ → S.X₂ → S.X₃ → 0` in `Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology X)
372	(ModuleCat kbar)` with `S.X₁` flasque, the section-level map
373	`(S.g.val.app (op U)).hom : S.X₂.val.obj (op U) → S.X₃.val.obj (op U)`
374	is surjective for every open `U`.
375	
376	**Proof sketch** (Hartshorne II.1 Ex 1.16(b), Zorn's lemma):
377	Let `t ∈ S.X₃.val.obj (op U)`. Consider pairs `(V, s)` where `V ⊆ U` is
378	open and `s ∈ S.X₂.val.obj (op V)` restricts to `t|_V`. Order by
379	extension. Any chain has an upper bound (sheaf gluing condition on
380	`S.X₂`). By Zorn's lemma, a maximal element `(V₀, s₀)` exists. Suppose
381	`V₀ ≠ U`. Pick `x ∈ U \ V₀`. Stalkwise, `S.X₂_x → S.X₃_x` is surjective
382	(SES in the abelian sheaf category preserves stalks). So in some
383	neighborhood `W` of `x` (after shrinking), there is
384	`s'_W ∈ S.X₂.val.obj (op W)` with image `t|_W`. On `V₀ ∩ W`,
385	`s₀ - s'_W` lies in `S.X₁.val.obj (op (V₀ ∩ W))` (since both map to
386	`t|_{V₀∩W}`). Use flasqueness of `S.X₁` to extend the difference to
387	`α ∈ S.X₁.val.obj (op W)`. Set `s'' := s'_W + α`. Then `s''` agrees with
388	`s₀` on `V₀ ∩ W`. By sheaf gluing on `S.X₂`, get a unique
389	`s''' ∈ S.X₂.val.obj (op (V₀ ∪ W))` extending both. This contradicts
390	maximality. Hence `V₀ = U`.
391	
392	**iter-194 Lane H prover dispatch** — body closed via the **forget₂**
393	trick: convert the project-side `Scheme.IsFlasque` data to Mathlib's
394	`TopCat.Sheaf.IsFlasque` predicate (on `AddCommGrpCat`-valued sheaves)
395	via the `sheafCompose (Opens.grothendieckTopology X) (forget₂
396	(ModuleCat kbar) AddCommGrpCat)` functor, then invoke Mathlib's
397	`TopCat.Sheaf.IsFlasque.epi_of_shortExact` which is exactly this lemma
398	at the `AddCommGrpCat`-valued sheaf level. The underlying function of
399	the section-level morphism is the same before and after `forget₂`, so
400	`Function.Surjective` at the `ModuleCat kbar` level transfers from
401	`Epi` at the `AddCommGrpCat` level via `ModuleCat.epi_iff_surjective`
402	respectively `AddCommGrpCat.epi_iff_surjective`. Blueprint reference:
403	out-of-scope subsection of `thm:H1_vanishing_flasque` (the
404	Hartshorne II.1 Ex 1.16(b) input). -/
405	theorem Scheme.IsFlasque.shortExact_app_surjective
406	    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] {X : TopCat.{u}}
407	    [HasSheafify (Opens.grothendieckTopology X) (ModuleCat.{u} kbar)]
408	    {S : CategoryTheory.ShortComplex
409	      (Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology X) (ModuleCat.{u} kbar))}
410	    (hS : S.ShortExact)
411	    (hF : Scheme.IsFlasque S.X₁)
412	    (U : TopologicalSpace.Opens X) :
413	    Function.Surjective ((S.g.hom.app (Opposite.op U)).hom) := by
414	  -- Set up the forget₂ functor at the sheaf level.
415	  set Fforget := CategoryTheory.sheafCompose (Opens.grothendieckTopology X)
416	    (CategoryTheory.forget₂ (ModuleCat.{u} kbar) AddCommGrpCat.{u}) with hFforget
417	  -- Build the AddCommGrpCat-valued ShortComplex via `S.map Fforget`.
418	  -- (Lean accepts this because `Fforget` has `PreservesZeroMorphisms` via the
419	  -- `sheafCompose_preservesZero` instance above.)
420	  set SAb : CategoryTheory.ShortComplex
421	      (Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology X) AddCommGrpCat.{u}) :=
422	    S.map Fforget with hSAb
423	  -- The IsFlasque hypothesis transfers across `forget₂`.
424	  haveI : TopCat.Sheaf.IsFlasque SAb.X₁ := hF.toAddCommGrpCat
425	  -- Mathlib's `IsFlasque.epi_of_shortExact` applies once we exhibit
426	  -- `SAb.ShortExact`. We decompose into its three components.
427	  -- (a) `Mono SAb.f` — provable axiom-clean using
428	  --     `Sheaf.Hom.mono_of_presheaf_mono` + `NatTrans.mono_of_mono_app`
429	  --     (since `forget₂` preserves underlying-function-level mono).
430	  -- (b) `Epi SAb.g` — provable axiom-clean using
431	  --     `Sheaf.isLocallySurjective_iff_epi'` + the fact that
432	  --     `IsLocallySurjective` only depends on underlying types (so transfers
433	  --     across `forget₂`).
434	  -- (c) `SAb.Exact` — the remaining gap. Requires
435	  --     `(sheafCompose forget₂).PreservesHomology` (specifically
436	  --     preservation of kernels and cokernels in the sheaf category).
437	  haveI hMono_SAb_f : CategoryTheory.Mono SAb.f := by
438	    haveI : CategoryTheory.Mono S.f := hS.mono_f
439	    haveI hmono : CategoryTheory.Mono (Fforget.map S.f).hom := by
440	      change CategoryTheory.Mono
441	        (CategoryTheory.Functor.whiskerRight S.f.hom
442	          (CategoryTheory.forget₂ (ModuleCat.{u} kbar) AddCommGrpCat.{u}))
443	      apply CategoryTheory.NatTrans.mono_of_mono_app
444	    change CategoryTheory.Mono (Fforget.map S.f)
445	    exact CategoryTheory.Sheaf.Hom.mono_of_presheaf_mono _ _ _
446	  haveI hEpi_SAb_g : CategoryTheory.Epi SAb.g := by
447	    haveI : CategoryTheory.Epi S.g := hS.epi_g
448	    change CategoryTheory.Epi (Fforget.map S.g)
449	    rw [← CategoryTheory.Sheaf.isLocallySurjective_iff_epi']

import Mathlib

open CategoryTheory CategoryTheory.Limits Functor

universe u

-- Strategy: PreservesFiniteLimits Fforget → (S.map Fforget).Exact for S short exact.
-- Provide the local PreservesFiniteLimits instance:
instance my_sheafCompose_pf {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] {X : TopCat.{u}} :
    PreservesFiniteLimits (sheafCompose (Opens.grothendieckTopology X)
      (forget₂ (ModuleCat.{u} kbar) AddCommGrpCat.{u})) := by
  have heq :
    (sheafCompose (Opens.grothendieckTopology X)
        (forget₂ (ModuleCat.{u} kbar) AddCommGrpCat.{u}) ⋙
      sheafToPresheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology X) AddCommGrpCat.{u}) =
    (sheafToPresheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology X) (ModuleCat.{u} kbar) ⋙
      (Functor.whiskeringRight _ _ _).obj
        (forget₂ (ModuleCat.{u} kbar) AddCommGrpCat.{u})) := rfl
  haveI hcomp : PreservesFiniteLimits
      (sheafCompose (Opens.grothendieckTopology X)
        (forget₂ (ModuleCat.{u} kbar) AddCommGrpCat.{u}) ⋙
      sheafToPresheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology X) AddCommGrpCat.{u}) := by
    rw [heq]
    refine ⟨fun K _ _ => ?_⟩
    infer_instance
  exact preservesFiniteLimits_of_reflects_of_preserves _
    (sheafToPresheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology X) AddCommGrpCat.{u})

-- Now the additive instance (need to provide).
-- Already in the source file but let me check inferrable here:
instance my_sheafCompose_add {C : Type*} [Category C]
    (J : GrothendieckTopology C)
    {A B : Type*} [Category A] [Category B]
    [Preadditive A] [Preadditive B]
    (F : A ⥤ B) [F.Additive] [J.HasSheafCompose F] :
    (sheafCompose J F).Additive where
  map_add := by
    intro X Y f g
    apply (sheafToPresheaf J B).map_injective
    change Functor.whiskerRight (f + g).hom F =
      Functor.whiskerRight f.hom F + Functor.whiskerRight g.hom F
    ext c
    simp
    rfl

-- Try direct: from PreservesFiniteLimits, get exactness
example {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] {X : TopCat.{u}}
    {S : CategoryTheory.ShortComplex
      (CategoryTheory.Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology X) (ModuleCat.{u} kbar))}
    (hS : S.ShortExact) :
    (S.map (sheafCompose (Opens.grothendieckTopology X)
      (forget₂ (ModuleCat.{u} kbar) AddCommGrpCat.{u}))).Exact :=
  ((preservesFiniteLimits_iff_forall_exact_map_and_mono _).mp inferInstance S hS).1

/-- `PreservesZeroMorphisms` instance for `sheafCompose J F` when `F`
is additive (derived from the `Additive` instance above). -/
instance sheafCompose_preservesZero {C : Type*} [CategoryTheory.Category C]
    (J : CategoryTheory.GrothendieckTopology C)
    {A B : Type*} [CategoryTheory.Category A] [CategoryTheory.Category B]
    [CategoryTheory.Preadditive A] [CategoryTheory.Preadditive B]
    (F : A ⥤ B) [F.Additive] [J.HasSheafCompose F] :
    (CategoryTheory.sheafCompose J F).PreservesZeroMorphisms :=
  CategoryTheory.Functor.preservesZeroMorphisms_of_additive _

/-- **iter-195 Lane H prover dispatch — single helper for SAb.Exact closure.**
`PreservesFiniteLimits` instance for `sheafCompose J F` when `F` preserves
finite limits, `A` and `B` are abelian and have finite limits, and the
sheaf categories have finite limits.

The proof uses the canonical factorisation
`sheafCompose J F ⋙ sheafToPresheaf J B = sheafToPresheaf J A ⋙ (whiskeringRight _ _ _).obj F`
(by `rfl`): the right side preserves finite limits (each factor does), and
`sheafToPresheaf J B` reflects them (it is fully faithful, hence reflects isos),
so `sheafCompose J F` does too.

This is the single helper that closes the `SAb.Exact` gap in
`Scheme.IsFlasque.shortExact_app_surjective`: by
`Functor.preservesFiniteLimits_iff_forall_exact_map_and_mono`, a
finite-limit-preserving additive functor between abelian categories takes
short exact sequences to exact short complexes. -/
instance sheafCompose_preservesFiniteLimits
    {C : Type*} [CategoryTheory.Category C]
    (J : CategoryTheory.GrothendieckTopology C)
    {A B : Type*} [CategoryTheory.Category A] [CategoryTheory.Category B]
    [CategoryTheory.Limits.HasFiniteLimits A]
    [CategoryTheory.Limits.HasFiniteLimits B]
    (F : A ⥤ B) [CategoryTheory.Limits.PreservesFiniteLimits F]
    [J.HasSheafCompose F] :
    CategoryTheory.Limits.PreservesFiniteLimits
      (CategoryTheory.sheafCompose J F) := by

# AlgebraicJacobian/RiemannRoch/H1Vanishing.lean — iter-195 Lane H

## Session summary

- **HARD BAR MET (axiom-clean closure)**: `Scheme.IsFlasque.shortExact_app_surjective` —
  closed the residual `SAb.Exact` gap at the former L462. Sorry count for this
  lemma: 1 → 0. Helper used: **1** (well under the 2-helper budget).
- **PUSH-BEYOND on `HModule_flasque_eq_zero`**: NOT achieved — propagation
  remains blocked on the upstream `IsFlasque.injective_flasque` (Hartshorne III
  Lemma 2.4) typed sorry at L572, which itself requires the `j_!`
  extension-by-zero functor (Mathlib `b80f227` does not ship it; ~100–150 LOC).
- **File status**: 4 sorries → **3 sorries** (net −1). Build GREEN.
  `Scheme.IsFlasque.shortExact_app_surjective` axiom-clean
  (`{propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound}`).
- **Remaining typed sorries**: `IsFlasque.constant_of_irreducible` (L138),
  `IsFlasque.injective_flasque` (L572), `skyscraperSheaf_eq_pushforward_const`
  (L760). All three are Tier-3 substrate gaps unrelated to the iter-195 Lane H
  closure target.

## sheafCompose_preservesFiniteLimits (NEW instance, helper #1, line ~340)

- **Approach**: project-local `instance` proving
  `PreservesFiniteLimits (sheafCompose J F)` for any `F : A ⥤ B` between
  abelian categories with finite limits, when `F` preserves finite limits and
  `J.HasSheafCompose F`.
- **Proof technique**: factor through `sheafToPresheaf J B`:
  ```
  sheafCompose J F ⋙ sheafToPresheaf J B
    = sheafToPresheaf J A ⋙ (whiskeringRight _ _ _).obj F      (by rfl)
  ```
  The right side preserves finite limits (each factor does, via
  `whiskeringRight_preservesLimitsOfShape` + `sheafToPresheaf` preserves
  finite limits). Since `sheafToPresheaf J B` is fully faithful and hence
  reflects isomorphisms, `preservesFiniteLimits_of_reflects_of_preserves`
  transports preservation back to `sheafCompose J F`.
- **Result**: RESOLVED — axiom-clean.

## SAb.Exact closure (formerly L462 sorry, now line ~497)

- **Approach**: invoke `Functor.preservesFiniteLimits_iff_forall_exact_map_and_mono`
  in the forward direction. The lemma states that for an additive functor `F`
  between abelian categories, `PreservesFiniteLimits F ↔ ∀ S, S.ShortExact →
  (S.map F).Exact ∧ Mono (F.map S.f)`. We have:
  * `Fforget = sheafCompose (Opens.grothendieckTopology X) (forget₂
    (ModuleCat kbar) AddCommGrpCat)` is additive (via the existing
    `sheafCompose_additive` instance).
  * `Fforget` preserves finite limits (via the new
    `sheafCompose_preservesFiniteLimits` instance — `forget₂ ModuleCat AddCommGrpCat`
    preserves all limits, which transports to the sheaf level by our helper).
  * `S.ShortExact` is the lemma's hypothesis.
  The `.1` projection then yields `(S.map Fforget).Exact = SAb.Exact`.
- **Result**: RESOLVED — axiom-clean (verified via `lean_verify`
  on `Scheme.IsFlasque.shortExact_app_surjective`).
- **Note**: This route avoids the substantial PreservesFiniteColimits +
  PreservesSheafification machinery that would otherwise be needed if we
  went through `Functor.preservesHomologyOfExact` → `Functor.PreservesHomology`
  → `ShortComplex.Exact.map`. The key insight is that for `S.ShortExact`
  inputs, exact preservation requires only the LEFT half (PreservesFiniteLimits)
  on the additive functor side.

## HModule_flasque_eq_zero push-beyond (NOT achieved)

- **Approach 1**: attempt to make `HModule_flasque_eq_zero` axiom-clean by
  closing the upstream `IsFlasque.injective_flasque` sorry — FAILED because
  Hartshorne III Lemma 2.4 requires the `j_!` extension-by-zero functor for
  sheaves of modules, which Mathlib snapshot `b80f227` does not ship
  (~100–150 LOC project-side build). Out of budget for iter-195.
- **Approach 2**: NONE attempted — alternative routes (e.g., a direct
  Godement-style resolution argument bypassing flasqueness) would be a
  different and larger project.
- **Next step (iter-196+)**: USER escalation OR Mathlib upstream PR for `j_!`
  on sheaves of modules. Per the standing iter-195 strategic context, this
  is analogous to the Lane A.3.i `NEEDS_MATHLIB_GAP_FILL` decision.

## Why I stopped

**Real progress**: closed 1 axiom-clean declaration on the iter-195 critical
path (`Scheme.IsFlasque.shortExact_app_surjective`, formerly the residual L462
`SAb.Exact` sorry) via a single project-local helper instance
(`sheafCompose_preservesFiniteLimits`). HARD BAR MET; helper budget = 1/2
(well under cap). Push-beyond on `HModule_flasque_eq_zero` was blocked by the
unrelated `injective_flasque` Tier-3 sorry (Hartshorne III Lemma 2.4 / `j_!`
gap) which would itself require a multi-iter substrate build, and lies outside
the explicit iter-195 helper budget.

## Blueprint marker recommendations

- `lem:isFlasque_pushforward` — already `\leanok` (declaration was
  pre-existing axiom-clean).
- `lem:isFlasque_constant_irreducible` — unchanged (still typed sorry).
- `lem:flasque_cokernel_short_exact` — already `\leanok`.
- `lem:ext_succ_zero_of_injective_lower_zero` — already `\leanok`.
- `thm:H1_vanishing_flasque` — still has sorryAx via `injective_flasque`;
  recommend NO `\leanok` until that closes.
- The new project-local instance `sheafCompose_preservesFiniteLimits` is
  not blueprint-pinned (it's an unnamed structural infrastructure helper);
  no marker change needed.
